rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING VS. GRISEZ BUCHANAN, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renotice Of Hearing On Motion For Monetary And Nonmonetary Expenses And Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees Against Plaintiff Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 26, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT GRISEZ BUCHANAN, LLC, JEROLD GRISEZ Renotice Of Hearing On Motion For Monetary And Nonmonetary Expenses And Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees Against Plaintiff Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 continued to October 3, 2019. By no later than September 27, 2019 Plaintiff to provide a supplemental declaration addressing the questions listed below. Do no provide any additional argument if the requested responses is "Yes or No." 1. Is an existing and current as of the date of the filing (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of any of the alleged in the Complaint and First Amended Complaint.? Yes or No. 2. Is an existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of request for injunction sought by Plaintiff? Yes or No. 3. Is evidence of existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of any of the claims (including relief sought, such as injunction) alleged in the Complaint and First Amended Complaint. Yes or No 4. Is an existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a factor considered by DFEH in filing and maintaining an action against a landlord. Yes or No. 5. Is evidence of existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a factor considered by DFEH in filing and maintaining an action against a landlord. Yes or No. 6. May DFEH maintain an action for discrimination and civil rights violation (including injunction) without evidence of existing disability requiring accommodation during the pendency of litigation? Yes or No. If answer to all the questions above is "No," the following questions do not require responses. If any question above is answered in the affirmative, please provide responses to the following questions: 1. List (and attach) all the medical evidence of existing and current disability (re: support animal) relied upon at the time of the filing of the Complaint. 2. List (and attach) all the medical evidence of existing and current disability (re: support animal) relied upon at the time of the filing of the First Amended Complaint. Defendant may file a response declaration addressing the same questions by no later than October 1, 2019. =(501/REQ)
